methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: User sends tokens to MagicLP contract
sellBase/sellQuote reads balance delta as input
transformations: Input tokens -> PMM pricing -> gross output
Gross output - lpFee - mtFee = net output
data_flows.trust_changes: tx.origin is used instead of msg.sender for fee calculation, bypassing contract-level fee customization
exposure_risks: tx.origin usage allows fee manipulation via phishing
manipulation_risks: tx.origin in fee calculation allows attacker to proxy calls through victim EOA for different fee rates
Front-running the swap by sandwiching with large trades

path: For each user: check _isSolvent with fresh rate
Calculate collateralShare from borrowAmount * LIQUIDATION_MULTIPLIER * exchangeRate
Transfer MIM from liquidator to cauldron
transformations: borrowAmount -> collateralShare via exchange rate and liquidation multiplier
data_flows.trust_changes: Swapper is freely chosen by caller; swapper can interact with BentoBox during callback
exposure_risks: Swapper callback can observe and manipulate BentoBox state
manipulation_risks: allBorrowShare is computed twice - swapper can manipulate BentoBox totals between the two computations, causing the second toShare to differ from first
Liquidator can choose a malicious swapper that manipulates BentoBox state

path: Fetch base and quote oracle answers
Call _getReserves() for base and quote reserves
transformations: LP price = min(basePrice, quotePrice) * totalReserveValue / totalSupply
sinks: Return value used by Cauldron for solvency checks
data_flows.trust_changes: Oracle answer directly affects collateral valuation and liquidation threshold
exposure_risks: CRITICAL: _getReserves() does not return values - returns (0, 0) by default, making latestAnswer() return 0
manipulation_risks: _getReserves() bug causes oracle to always return 0, making all LP collateral worthless in any Cauldron using this oracle
Even if _getReserves was fixed: reserves can be manipulated via flash loans to inflate/deflate LP price

path: _updateRewards updates global rewardPerToken for all tokens
Tokens transferred from operator to contract
Remaining rewards from current period added to new amount
rewardRate = total amount / remaining time to next epoch
Users accrue rewards proportional to balanceOf(user) / totalSupply
transformations: amount + leftover = total reward for period
user balance * rewardPerToken delta / 1e18 = user earned
data_flows.trust_changes: Operator controls reward rate; reward distribution delays 1 epoch for locked rewards
exposure_risks: Reward tokens are locked for 1 epoch before claimable
manipulation_risks: Large stake right before reward notification to capture disproportionate share
Flash stake attack mitigated by epoch delay on reward claims

path: Verify ready state and not already claimed
Call staking.stakeFor to stake LP shares for user
data_flows.trust_changes: User deposits converted to LP stakes; no ability to withdraw original tokens after bootstrap
manipulation_risks: MIM and USDB locked amounts are summed directly despite potentially different values (I = 0.998), giving slight advantage to USDB depositors
totalPoolShares may not equal totalLocked in value terms due to pool pricing

path: Convert amount to shares or shares to amount via Rebase
Transfer tokens from user or wrap ETH
data_flows.trust_changes: Deposit trust model: from == msg.sender, from == address(this) for skim, or approved masterContract
exposure_risks: Skim mode allows anyone to use contract's excess balance
manipulation_risks: First depositor can manipulate share ratio by sending tokens directly then depositing
Strategy balance included in _tokenBalanceOf can be manipulated by strategy

data_flows.source: User calling CauldronV4.cook with array of actions
path: value1/value2 carry return values between actions
ACTION_CALL allows arbitrary external calls (except blacklisted)
Solvency check at end if needed
transformations: _num function resolves int256 to uint256 using value1/value2 sentinel values
data_flows.trust_changes: ACTION_CALL delegates to arbitrary non-blacklisted contracts; return data is trusted
exposure_risks: ACTION_CALL can call arbitrary contracts with arbitrary data
manipulation_risks: Composable actions can be chained to create complex attack sequences
Return value from ACTION_CALL is trusted without validation

path: Transfer base and quote tokens to assetTo
Compute swap output and verify against borrowed amount
transformations: PMM pricing validates the returned amount covers borrowed + fees
data_flows.trust_changes: assetTo receives callback and can execute arbitrary logic
manipulation_risks: Flash loan can be used to manipulate pool state for oracle attacks
Callback could interact with other contracts that depend on pool state

core_rules: Users must remain solvent (collateral value >= borrow value * collateralization rate) after borrowing or removing collateral
Interest accrues continuously based on INTEREST_PER_SECOND on totalBorrow.elastic
LP shares are proportional to the minimum ratio of base/quote deposits relative to reserves
PMM pricing follows proactive market maker algorithm with constant I parameter
Locked staking provides boosted rewards proportional to lockingBoostMultiplerInBips
Reward claims are delayed by 1 epoch (locked until next epoch boundary)
BentoBox maintains share/amount ratio with minimum share balance of 1000
MagicLP dead shares (1001) are minted to address(0) on first deposit to prevent share ratio manipulation
economic_incentives: Liquidators receive collateral worth borrowAmount * LIQUIDATION_MULTIPLIER, incentivizing liquidation
10% of liquidation fee surplus goes to protocol (feesEarned), 90% to liquidator
LP providers earn trading fees proportional to their share
Maintainer earns separate mtFee on each swap
Locking tokens for 13 weeks provides lockingBoostMultiplerInBips / BIPS multiplier on staking rewards
Borrow opening fee charged as percentage of borrowed amount, accrues to protocol
governance_mechanisms: MasterContract owner can change interest rates, borrow limits, liquidation parameters
Implementation owner controls MagicLP parameters via setParameters
Factory owner can set implementation and maintainer fee model
Operators can notify rewards and process expired locks in LockingMultiRewards

edge_cases.scenario: MagicLpAggregator._getReserves() returns (0,0) instead of actual reserves
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should return pair's base and quote reserves for LP price calculation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Function declares local variables but has no return statement; unnamed return values default to (0,0)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Any Cauldron using MagicLpAggregator as oracle will see LP collateral as worthless (price = 0), causing immediate insolvency for all borrowers and blocking new borrows

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should validate that the NEW _feeBips doesn't exceed BIPS
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Validates the OLD feeBips state variable instead of the _feeBips parameter
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Fee operator can set feeBips to any value above BIPS (100%), extracting more than the full amount on fee calculations. First call always passes since initial feeBips is 0.

edge_cases.scenario: MagicLP swap functions use tx.origin for fee rate lookup
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fee rate should be based on the direct caller (msg.sender)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses tx.origin which is the EOA initiating the transaction
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Phishing attack: trick user into calling malicious contract that triggers swap; fee rate applied is for victim's EOA address rather than the contract. May bypass intended fee structures for contracts.

edge_cases.scenario: CauldronV4.liquidate computes allBorrowShare twice with swapper call in between
edge_cases.actual_behavior: First computation used as input to swapper, second computation used for actual transfer; swapper can change BentoBox totals between calls
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious swapper could manipulate BentoBox MIM totals between the two toShare calls, causing liquidator to pay different amount than expected

edge_cases.scenario: BlastOnboardingBoot._claimable sums MIM and USDB locked amounts directly
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should account for different token values (I = 0.998 means 1 MIM ≈ 0.998 USDB)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Sums raw amounts: userLocked = balances[user][MIM].locked + balances[user][USDB].locked
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Users depositing USDB get slightly fewer pool shares than their proportional value contribution since USDB is worth more than MIM. Conversely MIM depositors get slightly more shares than deserved.

edge_cases.scenario: MagicLP.buyShares when totalSupply > 0 but baseReserve == 0 or quoteReserve == 0
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should handle gracefully or revert with clear error
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Falls through both if/else-if branches; shares remains 0; _mint(to, 0) reverts with ErrMintAmountNotEnough
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No direct exploitation but poor error handling could confuse integrators

edge_cases.expected_behavior: lastLockIndex should always point to the most recently created lock
edge_cases.actual_behavior: After swap-and-pop, lastLockIndex may point to an older lock that was swapped into the position, causing _createLock to create new entries instead of appending
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Users could end up with more lock entries than expected, potentially hitting maxLocks limit prematurely and being unable to lock more tokens

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Reads masterContract.feeTo() which could return address(0); BentoBox transfer to address(0) would revert
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If feeTo not set on masterContract, withdrawFees reverts, locking fees

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should handle or give clear error
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint8 subtraction (quoteDecimals - baseDecimals) underflows and reverts in Solidity 0.8+
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Cannot create pools through Router where base token has more decimals than quote token, even if difference is small

edge_cases.scenario: CauldronV4 borrow limit check uses elastic for total but parts for per-address
edge_cases.actual_behavior: totalBorrow.elastic <= cap.total (includes accrued interest) while userBorrowPart <= cap.borrowPartPerAddress (does not include interest)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: As interest accrues, total limit becomes effectively lower while per-address limits remain constant in part terms. This could prevent new borrows when total interest pushes elastic above cap.

edge_cases.scenario: MagicLP.setParameters silently truncates _LP_FEE_RATE_, _K_, _I_ via unsafe casts
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Values should be stored at full precision or validated against cast limits
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Cast to uint64/uint64/uint128 respectively before storing in uint256 state variables
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: For valid input ranges (after validation checks), values fit within cast types. However, the implicit narrowing is fragile and could cause issues if MAX constants are increased.

edge_cases.scenario: BlastOnboarding proxy storage collision with BlastOnboardingBoot
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Proxy and implementation should have aligned storage layouts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: BlastOnboarding inherits BlastOnboardingData + Proxy; Boot inherits BlastOnboardingBootDataV1 (extends BlastOnboardingData) with new storage vars (pool, router, etc.)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If Proxy adds storage variables, they could collide with BlastOnboardingBootDataV1's new variables (pool, router, factory, etc.). OpenZeppelin's Proxy itself has no storage, but ERC1967 patterns should be verified.

assumption_violations: MagicLpAggregator assumes _getReserves returns valid data but it returns (0,0) due to missing return statement
CauldronV4 assumes BentoBox totals don't change during liquidation swap callback
BlastOnboardingBoot assumes MIM and USDB have equal value per unit for share distribution
MagicLP assumes tx.origin is an appropriate identifier for fee rate purposes
LockingMultiRewards assumes lastLockIndex always correctly points to the chronologically latest lock after processExpiredLocks
DegenBox assumes rebasing tokens are not used (explicitly documented) but BlastBox configures CLAIMABLE mode which is compatible
FeeCollectable assumes the validation in setFeeParameters checks the new value, but it checks the old value
game_theory_risks: Liquidators can choose swappers that manipulate BentoBox state to their advantage during liquidation callback
Staking rewards can be gamed by large stakers timing their locks around reward notification epochs
PMM pricing with constant I can be exploited by arbitrageurs when market price deviates from I
Flash loan in MagicLP allows atomic manipulation of reserves for oracle manipulation if oracles read pool state
Bootstrap share allocation favors MIM depositors over USDB depositors due to direct amount summation despite I < 1
integration_risks: BlastBox configuring tokens as CLAIMABLE assumes DegenBox accounting handles non-rebasing yield correctly; if tokens auto-rebase, share/amount ratio breaks
MagicLpAggregator oracle returning 0 would cause cascading failures in any Cauldron using LP as collateral
Blast precompile interactions could fail silently or change behavior on upgrades
FeeRateModel is an external dependency that could return arbitrary fee rates
Strategy contracts in DegenBox could report incorrect balances affecting all share calculations
calculation_errors: MagicLpAggregator._getReserves() returns (0,0) making latestAnswer() always 0 - division by pair.totalSupply() of (0 + 0) = 0
FeeCollectable.setFeeParameters checks 'feeBips > BIPS' instead of '_feeBips > BIPS', allowing any fee percentage on first set
BlastOnboardingBoot._claimable: (userMIM + userUSDB) * totalPoolShares / (totalMIM + totalUSDB) incorrectly treats tokens as same value

token_semantics.notes: MagicInternetMoney stablecoin; used as borrow asset in Cauldrons
Blast native USDB with rebasing yield; configured as CLAIMABLE in Blast contracts. Must NOT be set to AUTOMATIC in DegenBox/BlastBox
Blast native WETH with rebasing yield; configured as CLAIMABLE. AUTOMATIC mode would break BentoBox accounting
LP token for MIMSwap pools; decimals match base token decimals; 1001 dead shares minted to address(0) on initialization
Assumed to be standard ERC20; contract tracks balances via stakingTokenBalance rather than balanceOf

invariants.name: supply_conservation_bentobox
invariants.description: Total shares (base) must equal sum of all user balances for each token

invariants.name: cauldron_collateral_conservation
invariants.description: Total collateral share must equal sum of all user collateral shares

invariants.name: cauldron_borrow_conservation
invariants.description: Total borrow parts must equal sum of all user borrow parts

invariants.name: staking_supply_conservation
invariants.description: unlockedSupply == sum of all unlocked balances; lockedSupply == sum of all locked balances

invariants.name: staking_token_balance
invariants.description: stakingTokenBalance <= actual token balance; difference is recoverable excess

invariants.name: magiclp_reserve_consistency
invariants.description: After _sync(), reserves should equal actual balances. Between operations, actual balance >= reserve (excess can be used as input)

invariants.name: onboarding_balance_consistency
invariants.description: total should equal locked + unlocked for each token

invariants.name: solvency_invariant
invariants.description: For each user after any state change: either borrowPart == 0 or collateral value * CR >= borrow value * exchangeRate
